item.title: How Labour should deal with the backbench rebel alliance
item.description: Their influence is not inherently problematic — but such visible challenges to key policy proposals are

item.title: Iran and the rising perils of AI in warfare
item.description: Limits on the use of lethal autonomous weapons systems are urgent

item.title: Private credit has half the ingredients of a financial crisis
item.description: Worries arise that valuations shown on balance sheets don’t match reality

item.title: Lessons from an unwise banking photo shoot
item.description: An interview with four young men in finance dressed in Armani jackets and Hermès ties exposes several tensions

item.title: Should I go to the reunion?
item.description: If you’re even halfway happy with your life choices, I say go for it

item.title: The US consumer is OK
item.description: But if oil stays expensive . . .

item.title: How spreadbetter IG went from fringe to FTSE 100
item.description: IG shares are up almost 90 per cent since the arrival of Breon Corcoran as chief executive in early 2024.

item.title: Oil price shocks create many losers, but some winners too
item.description: Offsetting the higher prices, energy companies have to contend with disruption where they have people and assets

item.title: Should investors worry about a 2008-style shock?
item.description: From Iran to private credit, things are unnerving but the wider financial system is better prepared

item.title: Iran is playing a long game
item.description: An asymmetric war that brings the global economy to a standstill could exhaust the US and force a ceasefire

item.title: After all the rain, it’s time to mulch
item.description: The next two weekends are an opportunity to future-proof the garden by keeping moisture in the soil, blocking weeds and deterring slugs and snails

item.title: Why China’s renminbi push matters even if it never rivals the dollar
item.description: If renminbi settlement becomes cheaper and more trade migrates, firms will have less need to generate or hold US currency

item.title: How the Iran war will change global business
item.description: Damage from the conflict is already having an impact on corporate strategy

item.title: An uncomfortable truce in the AI platform wars
item.description: Anthropic and Microsoft have struck an alliance on agents but the tussle over the future of the technology will only intensify

item.title: Silence is not golden when it comes to science and AI
item.description: The scope of scientific inquiry is contracting as research focuses on data-rich problems

item.title: British politicians have the wrong kind of money on the brain
item.description: Westminster struggles to talk seriously about most topics and in particular about the economy

item.title: Senior loans, like airport lounges, are losing their elite status
item.description: First-lien is not so special any more due to changes in leveraged finance markets

item.title: Trump’s choices are only pushing China and Russia closer together
item.description: Beijing and Moscow look to one another for energy and economic security amid geopolitical turbulence

item.title: Worthwhile American initiative (on bank regulation)
item.description: For once, some constructive ideas are coming out of Washington

item.title: How Paris beat the car
item.description: Though chaotic, the city’s transition has become a global role model

item.title: China’s forgotten consumers
item.description: Plus more on private credit and BDCs

item.title: Trump’s Iran folly is handing Xi Jinping weapons in the trade war
item.description: Despite China being a huge oil importer, US mismanagement will give Beijing the upper hand in upcoming talks

item.title: Should you lie to your kids about their Junior Isas?
item.description: Parents worry about their children getting access to a lump sum at an early age — some choose to keep it a secret

item.title: Which chokepoint wins in a game of geoeconomic Top Trumps?
item.description: It depends on its damage to the target, potential for blowback and durability

item.title: America risks financial credibility with payments deficit obsession
item.description: Imposing tariffs in response threatens to create a problem where none exists
